channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Scott Morrison joins historic bronze collection in avenue of honour
item.description: A thinner ex-PM cracks jokes as his bust is revealed, making him the 30th Australian leader with a likeness in Ballarat's Prime Ministers Avenue.
item.dc:creator: Coco Veldkamp

media:group.media:description: Scott Morrison says the bronze has a strong resemblance even though he has "shed a few pounds since back then".

item.title: The journey of pollen to a plant in photos
item.description: Have you ever looked at a flower up close? Or wondered how plants are pollinated? Take a journey through the secret life of plants captured in stunning images by Australian botanists.
item.dc:creator: Chantelle Doyle

media:group.media:description: Drosera aberrans is native to New South Wales, South Australia, and Victoria.

item.title: Labor stalwart slams the 'view from Mar-a-Lago'
item.description: Labor and the Coalition are both acutely mindful of One Nation's rise and its determination to take a decisive role in a future conservative government.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Greber

media:group.media:description: Former treasurer Wayne Swan told Labor's true believers that the task ahead is about conviction.

item.title: Netanyahu gave a rare interview at a crucial time for the Middle East
item.description: The question for the Gulf states is whether they are happy for Israel to be the dominant player in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Laura Tingle

media:group.media:description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu gave a rare interview at a crucial time for the Middle East.

item.title: Cotton farmer stitches together fashion empire in regional Queensland
item.description: Queensland cotton farmer and entrepreneur Sam Coulton has spent decades diversifying beyond agriculture, building a fashion brand and tourism business in Goondiwindi while creating jobs and investing in the future of his rural town.
item.dc:creator: Pip Courtney

media:group.media:description: Sam Coulton started his clothing business 34 years ago.

item.title: Jane Caro reckons Australia is kidding itself about private schools
item.description: "There are absolutely no statistics that say that because you sent your kid to a private school they're going to do better in life."
item.dc:creator: Gina Rushton

media:group.media:description: Jane Caro's new book walks through the decades of policy decisions that have created inequality in Australia's education system.

item.title: Thornier questions lie ahead for antisemitism royal commission
item.description: Jewish Australians told the royal commission that antisemitism has spread into everyday life. But whether the inquiry can handle what it has promised to confront remains to be seen.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Pengilley and Phoebe Pin

media:group.media:description: The royal commission is examining the definition of antisemitism.

item.title: Most widespread May rain in a decade to reach all states
item.description: The most widespread May rain in a decade is about to sweep across Australia as the first north-west cloudband of 2026 becomes a reality.
item.dc:creator: Tom Saunders

media:group.media:description: Between Saturday and Tuesday, rain will extend across more than half the country.

item.title: Neo-Nazis fundraising to challenge laws that listed them as hate group
item.description: The leader of Neo-Nazi group National Socialist Network, Thomas Sewell, has appeared in an interview online alongside notorious US Neo-Nazi James Mason, who is listed as a terrorist entity by the Canadian government.
item.dc:creator: Matt Martino, Michael Workman, and Jonathan Hair

media:group.media:description: Despite claiming to have disbanded, members of the NSN continue to organise online.

item.title: New Hollywood film excites as Philippine culture hits the big screen
item.description: As animated film Forgotten Island gets ready to hit the big screen later this year, could it signal an end to Filipino typecasting in Hollywood?
item.dc:creator: Erielle Sudario

media:group.media:description: H.E.R. and Liza Soberano voice Jo and Raissa in upcoming film Forgotten Island.

item.title: Nic Cage makes the leap to TV in Spider-Man spin off
item.description: Plus, Shaun Micallef dives into Australia's gambling problem and the Stranger Things creators back another sci-fi mystery.
item.dc:creator: Velvet Winter

media:group.media:description: Nic Cage takes on his first leading television role in Spider-Noir.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of patron saints, the Friends finale and a 90s club banger
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

media:group.media:description: A dog is blessed by a priest at San Anton church in Madrid on Saint Anthony's Day, January 17, 2013. Dogs, cats, rabbits and even turtles, many dressed in their finest, trooped into churches across Spain in search of blessing. Saint Anthony is the patron saint of animals.

item.title: Zelenskyy vows retribution against Russia for missile strike that killed 24
item.description: Russia has unleashed its heaviest bombardment of the Ukrainian capital this year after a US-brokered ceasefire ended.

media:group.media:description: People lay flowers at the site of a Russian missile strike that killed 24 people.

item.title: Auckland cruises past Adelaide to reach maiden ALM grand final
item.description: Auckland becomes the first team from New Zealand to qualify for an A-League Men's season decider after making light work of Adelaide at Hindmarsh Stadium.

media:group.media:description: Auckland made light work of Adelaide in the second leg at Hindmarsh Stadium.

item.title: Five divers die following cave diving incident
item.description: The group was exploring a series of underwater caves at a depth of around 50 metres in Vaavu Atoll in the Maldives.

media:group.media:description: Monica Montefalcone and her daughter Giorgia Sommacal were among the five divers.

item.title: Swans hold on for gritty victory over Pies, Suns see off Port
item.description: The ladder-leading Sydney Swans scrape past an undermanned but plucky Collingwood side, as Gold Coast defeats Port Adelaide in Darwin.
item.dc:creator: Henry Hanson and Dean Bilton

media:group.media:description: Sydney edged out a plucky Collingwood side at the SCG.

item.title: Property developer bids $50,000 donation for lunch with deputy premier
item.description: The developer, David Trask, made the successful bid as a donation at lunch on Friday for the ruling LNP, held at the Brisbane Convention Centre and dubbed "Delivering for Queensland Industry Update".
item.dc:creator: Liam Walsh and Jack McKay

media:group.media:description: Queensland Deputy Premier Jarrod Bleijie oversees state agency Economic Development Queensland.

item.title: 'Barbecue stopper' Asian tiger mosquito detected on Australian mainland for the first time
item.description: The world's most invasive mosquito has previously been found at borders, islands, and ports, but has just been discovered in a remote mainland Queensland community.
item.dc:creator: Adam Stephen and Conor Byrne

media:group.media:description: The female Aedes albopictus, also known as the Asian tiger mosquito.

item.title: Regional incinerator for Sydney waste draws unanimous ire at inquiry
item.description: A parliamentary hearing into a proposed $600 million waste-to-energy plant at Tarago has heard widespread opposition and condemnation from the community.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Fuller

media:group.media:description: Community members and witnesses were staunch in their opposition to the waste-to-energy plant at the parliamentary inquiry hearing.

item.title: WA treasurer concedes $217 million cost of racetrack project may blow out
item.description: Despite assurances to the contrary, WA Treasurer Rita Saffioti has conceded it would be difficult for the government to stick to the $217.5 million budget for the Perth Park project.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Withers

media:group.media:description: Rita Saffioti speaks to reporters at the Burswood park site.

item.title: Shoplifting is so bad in this town that police are homing in
item.description: In the regional Victorian town of Shepparton, shoplifting and retail crime are so rife that police have launched a dedicated patrol unit to target repeat offenders.
item.dc:creator: Charmaine Manuel and Andrew Mangelsdorf

media:group.media:description: Police have launched a new focused operation on retail theft in Shepparton.

item.title: Chloroform in drain suspected as reason hospital ward shut, staff ill
item.description: An incident that prompted a Tasmanian hospital theatre to evacuate, and left several staff feeling ill, was possibly caused by chloroform, documents reveal.
item.dc:creator: Bec Pridham

media:group.media:description: According to RTI documents, it is hypothesised alcohol and chlorine were poured down the same sink, creating chloroform.

item.title: Bennett insists Latrell will be fit for Origin despite injury exit in loss to Dolphins
item.description: South Sydney coach Wayne Bennett says he expects Latrell Mitchell to take his place in State of Origin I despite the star centre hobbling off late in the Rabbitohs' Magic Round clash with the Dolphins.
item.dc:creator: Michael Doyle and Jon Healy

media:group.media:description: Latrell Mitchell hobbled off the field with a few minutes left in the game.

item.title: Neo-Nazi group banned in Australia under hate laws
item.description: White Australia will be listed as a hate group, making it illegal to support the organisation in any way.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke says the listing takes effect from midnight.

item.title: Ali Moore to hang up headphones as 774 Melbourne Drive host
item.description: Veteran broadcaster Ali Moore will step away from the show at the end of the month, with Charlie Pickering to join the program as the new host.

media:group.media:description: After a four-decade career in broadcasting, Ali Moore is stepping down as host of 774 ABC Melbourne Drive.
